seoTitle: How to Export QuickBooks Reports to Excel in Classic View
metaDescription: Learn how to export QuickBooks reports to Excel using Classic View, so you can pull financial data into spreadsheets for further analysis.
intro: If you're working in the Classic View layout, exporting reports to Excel still follows a clear path once you know where to look. This guide covers selecting the right report and getting it into Excel for further work.

steps.heading: Open the report you need
steps.body: Navigate to Reports in Classic View and select the specific report you want to export.

steps.heading: Set your filters and date range
steps.body: Adjust the report parameters so it reflects exactly the data you want before exporting.

steps.heading: Choose the export to Excel option
steps.body: Use the export button on the report screen to send the data to an Excel file.

steps.heading: Save and open the file
steps.body: Download the file to your computer and open it in Excel to confirm everything transferred correctly.

faq.question: Is exporting to Excel different in Classic View versus the new view?
faq.answer: The core export function is similar, but the menu layout and button placement can differ between the two views.

faq.question: Can I export any report to Excel?
faq.answer: Most standard and custom reports in QuickBooks include an export to Excel option.

faq.question: Do formulas transfer when exporting to Excel?
faq.answer: No, the export brings over static values, so any calculations you want beyond the report itself need to be added in Excel.

faq.question: What if the exported file doesn't open correctly?
faq.answer: Make sure you have a compatible version of Excel installed, and try re-downloading the file if it appears corrupted.
